AgentStatus.description: Status of a single agent in the dev server

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when agent was created

oneOf.description: Error if this agent has failed

resources.description: Resources deployed by this agent (keyed by resource name)

AlienError.description:
Canonical error container that provides a structured way to represent errors
with rich metadata including error codes, human-readable messages, context,
and chaining capabilities for error propagation.

This struct is designed to be both machine-readable and user-friendly,
supporting serialization for API responses and detailed error reporting
in distributed systems.

code.description:
A unique identifier for the type of error.

This should be a short, machine-readable string that can be used
by clients to programmatically handle different error types.
Examples: "NOT_FOUND", "VALIDATION_ERROR", "TIMEOUT"

context.description:
Additional diagnostic information about the error context.

This optional field can contain structured data providing more details
about the error, such as validation errors, request parameters that
caused the issue, or other relevant context information.

httpStatusCode.description:
HTTP status code for this error.

Used when converting the error to an HTTP response. If None, falls back to
the error type's default status code or 500.

internal.description:
Indicates if this is an internal error that should not be exposed to users.

When `true`, this error contains sensitive information or implementation
details that should not be shown to end-users. Such errors should be
logged for debugging but replaced with generic error messages in responses.

message.description:
Human-readable error message.

This message should be clear and actionable for developers or end-users,
providing context about what went wrong and potentially how to fix it.

retryable.description:
Indicates whether the operation that caused the error should be retried.

When `true`, the error is transient and the operation might succeed
if attempted again. When `false`, retrying the same operation is
unlikely to succeed without changes.

source.description:
The underlying error that caused this error, creating an error chain.

This allows for proper error propagation and debugging by maintaining
the full context of how an error occurred through multiple layers
of an application.

stack.description: Name of the stack being built

oneOf.description: Running build-time preflight checks and mutations

stack.description: Name of the stack being checked

oneOf.description: Resource build event (function, container, or worker)

relatedResources.description: All resource names sharing this build (for deduped container groups)

resourceName.description: Name of the resource being built

image.description: Name of the image being built
Name of the image being pushed

oneOf.description: Progress information for the push operation

stack.description: Name of the stack being pushed

resourceName.description: Name of the resource being pushed

oneOf.description: Creating a release on the platform

progress.description: Current progress/status line from the build output

nextState.description: The resulting state of the stack after the step.

suggestedDelayMs.description: An suggested duration to wait before executing the next step.

platform.description: Platform for which the template is being generated

agentId.description: ID of the agent being provisioned

releaseId.description: ID of the release being deployed to the agent

agentId.description: ID of the agent being updated

releaseId.description: ID of the new release being deployed to the agent

agentId.description: ID of the agent being deleted

releaseId.description: ID of the release that was running on the agent

oneOf.description: Starting a debug session for an agent

agentId.description: ID of the agent being debugged

strategyName.description: Name of the deployment strategy being used

stackName.description: Name of the stack being deployed
Name of the stack being tested
Name of the stack being cleaned up

strategyName.description: Name of the deployment strategy being used for cleanup

stackName.description: Name of the stack being cleaned up

strategyName.description: Name of the deployment strategy being used for cleanup

roleArn.description: ARN of the role to assume

oneOf.description: Emptying S3 buckets before stack deletion

bucketNames.description: Names of the S3 buckets being emptied

AlienEvent.description: Represents all possible events in the Alien system

ArtifactRegistry.description:
Represents an artifact registry for storing container images and other build artifacts.
This is a high-level wrapper resource that provides a cloud-agnostic interface over
AWS ECR, GCP Artifact Registry, and Azure Container Registry.

# Platform Mapping
- **AWS**: Implicitly exists as the AWS account and region
- **GCP**: Explicitly configured per project and location (Artifact Registry API enabled)
- **Azure**: Explicitly provisioned Azure Container Registry instance

The actual repository management and permissions are handled through the bindings API.

id.description:
Identifier for the artifact registry. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

ArtifactRegistryOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned ArtifactRegistry.

pullRole.description:
Role/principal identifier for pull-only access.
- AWS: IAM role ARN (e.g., "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/my-stack-my-registry-pull")
- GCP: Service account email (e.g., "my-stack-my-registry-pull@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com")
- Azure: Managed identity resource ID (e.g., "/subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/.../providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/my-registry-pull")

pushRole.description:
Role/principal identifier for push and pull access.
- AWS: IAM role ARN (e.g., "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/my-stack-my-registry-push")
- GCP: Service account email (e.g., "my-stack-my-registry-push@my-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com")
- Azure: Managed identity resource ID (e.g., "/subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/.../providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/my-registry-push")

registryEndpoint.description:
The registry endpoint for docker operations.
- AWS: ECR registry URL (e.g., "123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-west-2.amazonaws.com")
- GCP: Artifact Registry URL (e.g., "us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project")
- Azure: Container registry login server (e.g., "myregistry.azurecr.io")

registryId.description:
The platform-specific registry identifier.
- AWS: Account and region (e.g., "123456789012:us-west-2")
- GCP: Full registry name (e.g., "projects/my-project/locations/us-central1")
- Azure: Registry resource ID (e.g., "/subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/.../providers/Microsoft.ContainerRegistry/registries/myregistry")

AwsManagementConfig.description: AWS management configuration extracted from stack settings

managingRoleArn.description: The managing AWS IAM role ARN that can assume cross-account roles

binding.description: How to bind the permissions (stack vs resource scope)

AzureManagementConfig.description: Azure management configuration extracted from stack settings

managementPrincipalId.description: The principal ID of the service principal in the management account

managingTenantId.description: The managing Azure Tenant ID for cross-tenant access

binding.description: How to bind the permissions (stack vs resource scope)

BaseResource.description: Resource that can hold any resource type in the Alien system. All resources share common 'type' and 'id' fields with additional type-specific properties.

id.description: The unique identifier for this specific resource instance. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]). Maximum 64 characters.

BaseResourceOutputs.description: Resource outputs that can hold output data for any resource type in the Alien system. All resource outputs share a common 'type' field with additional type-specific output properties.

size.description: Size of the body in bytes

oneOf.description: Pre-signed request for retrieving the body (for deployments)

storagePutUsed.description: Indicates storage upload was used for response submission

BodySpec.description: Body specification supporting inline and storage modes

Build.description:
Represents a build resource that executes bash scripts to build code.
Builds are designed to be stateless and can be triggered on-demand to compile,
test, or package application code.

computeType.description: Amount of compute resources allocated to the build.

environment.description: Key-value pairs to set as environment variables for the build.

id.description:
Identifier for the build resource. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

links.description: List of resource references this build depends on.

permissions.description:
Permission profile name that defines the permissions granted to this build.
This references a profile defined in the stack's permission definitions.

computeType.description: Amount of compute resources allocated to the build.

environment.description: Key-value pairs to set as environment variables for the build.

image.description: Base container image to use for the build environment.

oneOf.description: Optional monitoring configuration for sending build logs

script.description: Bash script to execute for the build.

timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum execution time for the build in seconds.

BuildOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned Build.

identifier.description: The platform-specific build project identifier (ARN for AWS, project ID for GCP, resource ID for Azure).

CommandState.description: Command states in the ARC protocol lifecycle

Container.description:
Container resource for running long-running container workloads.

A Container defines a deployable unit that runs on a ContainerCluster.
Horizon handles scheduling replicas across machines, autoscaling based on
various metrics, and service discovery.

## Example

```rust
use alien_core::{Container, ContainerCode, ResourceSpec, ContainerAutoscaling, ContainerPort, ExposeProtocol};

let container = Container::new("api".to_string())
    .cluster("compute".to_string())
    .code(ContainerCode::Image {
        image: "myapp:latest".to_string(),
    })
    .cpu(ResourceSpec { min: "0.5".to_string(), desired: "1".to_string() })
    .memory(ResourceSpec { min: "512Mi".to_string(), desired: "1Gi".to_string() })
    .port(8080)
    .expose_port(8080, ExposeProtocol::Http)
    .autoscaling(ContainerAutoscaling {
        min: 2,
        desired: 3,
        max: 10,
        target_cpu_percent: Some(70.0),
        target_memory_percent: None,
        target_http_in_flight_per_replica: Some(100),
        max_http_p95_latency_ms: None,
    })
    .permissions("container-execution".to_string())
    .build();
```

cluster.description:
ContainerCluster resource ID that this container runs on.
If None, will be auto-assigned by ContainerClusterMutation at deployment time.

id.description:
Unique identifier for the container.
Must be DNS-compatible: lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens.

memory.description: Memory resource requirements (must use Ki/Mi/Gi/Ti suffix)

oneOf.description: Persistent storage configuration (only for stateful containers)

pool.description:
Capacity group to run on (must exist in the cluster)
If not specified, containers are scheduled to any available group.

ports.description: Container ports to expose (at least one required)

replicas.description: Fixed replica count (for stateful containers or stateless without autoscaling)

stateful.description: Whether container is stateful (gets stable ordinals, optional persistent volumes)

desired.description: Initial desired replicas at container creation

maxHttpP95LatencyMs.description: Maximum acceptable p95 HTTP latency in milliseconds

targetCpuPercent.description: Target CPU utilization percentage for scaling (default: 70%)

targetHttpInFlightPerReplica.description: Target in-flight HTTP requests per replica

targetMemoryPercent.description: Target memory utilization percentage for scaling (default: 80%)

src.description: The source directory to build from

ContainerCode.description: Specifies the source of the container's executable code.

ContainerOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned Container.

oneOf.description:
Load balancer endpoint information for DNS management (optional).
Used by the DNS controller to create custom domain mappings.
Optional exposure protocol (if None, port is internal-only)

CreateCommandRequest.description: Request to create a new ARC command

oneOf.description: Storage upload info (only for storage mode)

CustomCertificateConfig.description: Platform-specific certificate references for custom domains.

CustomDomainConfig.description: Custom domain configuration for a single resource.

domain.description: Fully qualified domain name to use.

DeploymentModel.description: Deployment model: how updates are delivered to the remote environment.

DeploymentStatus.description: Deployment status in the deployment lifecycle

DevStatus.description: Overall status of the dev server

agents.description: Agents being managed by this dev server (keyed by agent name)

oneOf.description: Global error if dev server itself has an error

lastUpdated.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last status update

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when dev server started

DomainSettings.description:
Domain configuration for the stack.

When `custom_domains` is set, the specified resources use customer-provided
domains and certificates. Otherwise, Alien auto-generates domains.

customDomains.description: Custom domain configuration per resource ID.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event was created

parentId.description: Parent event ID if this is a child event

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the event was updated

EventChange.description: Represents a change to an event

oneOf.description: Event has no specific state (simple events)

EventState.description: Represents the state of an event

Function.description:
Represents a serverless function that executes code in response to triggers or direct invocations.
Functions are the primary compute resource in serverless applications, designed to be stateless and ephemeral.

arcEnabled.description:
Whether the function can be invoked via ARC (Alien Remote Call) protocol from the control plane.
When enabled, the necessary queue infrastructure is automatically created for the target platform.

code.description: Code for the function, either a pre-built image or source code to be built.

concurrencyLimit.description:
Maximum number of concurrent executions allowed for the function.
None means platform default applies.

environment.description: Key-value pairs to set as environment variables for the function.

id.description:
Identifier for the function. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

oneOf.description: Controls network accessibility of the function.

links.description: List of resource references this function depends on.

memoryMb.description:
Memory allocated to the function in megabytes (MB).
Constraints: 128‑32768 MB (platform-specific limits may apply)
Default: 256

permissions.description:
Permission profile name that defines the permissions granted to this function.
This references a profile defined in the stack's permission definitions.

oneOf.description:
Optional readiness probe configuration.
Only applicable for functions with Public ingress.
When configured, the probe will be executed after provisioning/update to verify the function is ready.

timeoutSeconds.description:
Maximum execution time for the function in seconds.
Constraints: 1‑3600 seconds (platform-specific limits may apply)
Default: 30

triggers.description:
List of triggers that define what events automatically invoke this function.
If empty, the function is only invokable directly via HTTP calls or platform-specific invocation APIs.
When configured, the function will be automatically invoked when any of the specified trigger conditions are met.

src.description: The source directory to build from

FunctionCode.description:
Specifies the source of the function's executable code.
This can be a pre-built container image or source code that the system will build.

FunctionOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned Function.

oneOf.description:
Load balancer endpoint information for DNS management (optional).
Used by the DNS controller to create custom domain mappings.

url.description: The invocation URL (if applicable, e.g., for public ingress or specific platforms).

oneOf.description: Function triggered by queue messages (always 1 message per invocation)
Function triggered by storage events (future implementation)
Function triggered by scheduled events (future implementation)

FunctionTrigger.description: Defines what triggers a function execution

GcpManagementConfig.description: GCP management configuration extracted from stack settings

serviceAccountEmail.description: Service account email for management roles

binding.description: How to bind the permissions (stack vs resource scope)

failureThreshold.description: Number of consecutive failures before marking replica unhealthy

method.description: HTTP method to use for health check

port.description: Port to check (defaults to container port if not specified)

HeartbeatsMode.description: How heartbeat health checks are handled.

HttpMethod.description: HTTP method for readiness probe requests.

ImagePullCredentials.description: Image pull credentials for container registries

Ingress.description: Controls network accessibility of the function.

Kv.description:
Represents a key-value storage resource that provides a minimal, platform-agnostic API
compatible across DynamoDB, Firestore, Redis, and Azure Table Storage.

id.description:
Identifier for the KV store. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

KvOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned KV store.

endpoint.description: Platform-specific endpoint URL for direct access (if applicable).

identifier.description: Platform-specific identifier (e.g., DynamoDB table ARN, Redis endpoint).

storeName.description: The name of the KV store (may be platform-specific).

maxLeases.description: Maximum number of leases to acquire

leases.description: Acquired leases (empty array if none available)

LifecycleRule.description: Defines a rule for managing the lifecycle of objects within a storage bucket.

days.description: Number of days after which objects matching the rule expire.

prefix.description: Optional prefix to filter objects the rule applies to. If None, applies to all objects.

LoadBalancerEndpoint.description:
Load balancer endpoint information for DNS management.
This is optional metadata used by the DNS controller to create domain mappings.

dnsName.description: The DNS name of the load balancer endpoint (e.g., ALB DNS, API Gateway domain).

hostedZoneId.description: AWS Route53 hosted zone ID (for ALIAS records). Only set on AWS.

ManagementConfig.description:
Management configuration for different cloud platforms.

Platform-derived configuration for cross-account/cross-tenant access.
This is NOT user-specified - it's derived from the Agent Manager's ServiceAccount.

oneOf.description:
Auto-derived permissions only (default)
Uses resource lifecycles to determine management permissions:
- Frozen resources: `<type>/management`
- Live/LiveOnSetup resources: `<type>/provision`

ManagementPermissions.description: Management permissions configuration for stack management access

MessagePayload.description: JSON-first message payload that supports both structured JSON and UTF-8 text

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers to include in requests to the monitoring endpoint

logsUri.description: Optional URI path for logs (defaults to "/v1/logs")

tlsEnabled.description: Whether to enable TLS/HTTPS (defaults to true)

tlsVerify.description: Whether to verify TLS certificates (defaults to true)

oneOf.description:
Use the cloud provider's default VPC/network.

Designed for fast dev/test provisioning. No isolated VPC is created, so there
is nothing to wait for or clean up. VMs receive ephemeral public IPs for internet
access — no NAT gateway is provisioned.

- **AWS**: Discovers the account's default VPC. Subnets are public with auto-assigned IPs.
- **GCP**: Discovers the project's `default` network and regional subnet. Instance
  templates include an `AccessConfig` to assign an ephemeral external IP.
- **Azure**: Azure has no default VNet, so one is created along with a NAT Gateway.
  VMs stay private and use NAT for egress.

Not recommended for production. Use `Create` instead.

Create a new isolated VPC/VNet with a managed NAT gateway.

All networking infrastructure is provisioned by Alien and cleaned up on delete.
VMs use private IPs only; all outbound traffic routes through the NAT gateway.

Recommended for production deployments.

cidr.description:
VPC/VNet CIDR block. If not specified, auto-generated from stack ID
to reduce conflicts (e.g., "10.{hash}.0.0/16").

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VPC (AWS).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (NAT, proxy, VPN, etc.).

public_subnet_ids.description: IDs of public subnets (required for public ingress)

security_group_ids.description: Optional security group IDs to use

vpc_id.description: The ID of the existing VPC

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VPC (GCP).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (Cloud NAT, proxy, VPN, etc.).

network_name.description: The name of the existing VPC network

subnet_name.description: The name of the subnet to use

oneOf.description:
Use an existing VNet (Azure).

Alien validates the references but creates no networking infrastructure.
The customer is responsible for routing and egress (NAT Gateway, proxy, VPN, etc.).

private_subnet_name.description: Name of the private subnet within the VNet

public_subnet_name.description: Name of the public subnet within the VNet

vnet_resource_id.description: The full resource ID of the existing VNet

NetworkSettings.description:
Network configuration for the stack.

Controls how VPC/VNet networking is provisioned. Users configure this in
`StackSettings`; the Network resource itself is auto-generated by preflights.

## Egress policy

Container cluster VMs are configured for egress based on the mode:

- `UseDefault` → VMs get ephemeral public IPs (no NAT is provisioned)
- `Create` → VMs use private IPs; Alien provisions a NAT gateway for outbound access
- `ByoVpc*` / `ByoVnet*` → no public IPs assigned; customer manages egress

For production workloads, use `Create`. For fast dev/test iteration, `UseDefault` is
sufficient. For environments with existing VPCs, use the appropriate `ByoVpc*` variant.

PermissionGrant.description: Grant permissions for a specific cloud platform

PermissionProfile.description:
Permission profile that maps resources to permission sets
Key can be "*" for all resources or resource name for specific resource

PermissionSet.description: A permission set that can be applied across different cloud platforms

description.description: Human-readable description of what this permission set allows

id.description: Unique identifier for the permission set (e.g., "storage/data-read")

oneOf.description: Reference to a built-in permission set by name (e.g., "storage/data-read")

PermissionSetReference.description: Reference to a permission set - either by name or inline definition

PermissionsConfig.description: Combined permissions configuration that contains both profiles and management

management.description: Management permissions configuration for stack management access

profiles.description:
Permission profiles that define access control for compute services
Key is the profile name, value is the permission configuration

PersistentStorage.description: Persistent storage configuration for stateful containers.

iops.description: IOPS (for storage types that support it)

storageType.description: Storage type (e.g., "gp3", "io2" for AWS, "pd-ssd" for GCP)

throughput.description: Throughput in MiB/s (for storage types that support it)

PresignedOperation.description: The type of operation a presigned request performs

PresignedRequest.description:
A presigned request that can be serialized, stored, and executed later.
Hides implementation details for different storage backends.

backend.description: The storage backend this request targets

path.description: The path this request operates on

oneOf.description: HTTP-based request (AWS S3, GCP GCS, Azure Blob)

PresignedRequestBackend.description: Storage backend representation for different presigned request types

PushProgress.description: Progress information for image push operations

layersUploaded.description: Number of layers uploaded so far

totalLayers.description: Total number of layers to upload

Queue.description:
Represents a message queue resource with minimal, portable semantics.
Queue integrates with platform-native services (AWS SQS, GCP Pub/Sub, Azure Service Bus).

id.description:
Identifier for the queue. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

QueueMessage.description:
Standardized queue message structure used by alien-runtime

This structure contains commonly available metadata across all platforms
and a JSON-first payload that handles both structured data and plain text.

attemptCount.description: Delivery attempt count (from ApproximateReceiveCount/deliveryCount, if available)

QueueOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned Queue.

identifier.description: Platform-specific identifier or URL (e.g., SQS URL, Pub/Sub topic/subscription).

queueName.description: The name of the queue (platform-specific physical name).

ReadinessProbe.description:
Configuration for HTTP-based readiness probe.
This probe is executed after function provisioning/update to verify the function is ready to serve traffic.
Only works with functions that have Public ingress.

method.description:
HTTP method to use for the probe request.
Default: GET

path.description:
Path to request for the probe (e.g., "/health", "/ready").
Default: "/"

RemoteStackManagement.description:
Represents cross-account management access configuration for a stack deployed
on AWS, GCP, or Azure platforms. This resource sets up the necessary IAM/RBAC
configuration to allow another cloud account to manage the stack.

Maps to:
- AWS: Cross-account IAM role with management permissions
- GCP: Service account with management permissions and impersonation rights
- Azure: Lighthouse registration definition and assignment

This resource is automatically created for AWS, GCP, and Azure platforms
when the stack needs to be managed by another account. The management account
and identity information comes from the platform configuration.

id.description:
Identifier for the remote stack management. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

RemoteStackManagementOutputs.description:
Resource outputs for RemoteStackManagement.
Different platforms will provide different outputs based on their implementation.

accessConfiguration.description:
Platform-specific access configuration
For AWS: The role ARN to assume
For GCP: The service account email to impersonate
For Azure: The lighthouse registration assignment ID

managementResourceId.description:
Platform-specific management resource identifier
For AWS: The ARN of the created cross-account role
For GCP: The email of the created service account
For Azure: The resource ID of the lighthouse registration

ReplicaStatus.description: Status of a single container replica.

machineId.description: Machine ID the replica is running on

config.description: Resource configuration (can be any type of resource)

dependencies.description:
Additional dependencies for this resource beyond those defined in the resource itself.
The total dependencies are: resource.get_dependencies() + this list

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle management configuration for this resource

remoteAccess.description:
Enable remote bindings for this resource (BYOB use case).
When true, binding params are synced to StackState's `remote_binding_params`.
Default: false (prevents sensitive data in synced state).

ResourceLifecycle.description: Describes the lifecycle of a resource within a stack, determining how it's managed and deployed.

ResourceRef.description:
New ResourceRef that works with any resource type.
This can eventually replace the enum-based ResourceRef for full extensibility.

ResourceStatus.description: Represents the high-level status of a resource during its lifecycle.

ResourceType.description: Resource type identifier that determines the specific kind of resource. This field is used for polymorphic deserialization and resource-specific behavior.

maxInlineBytes.description: Maximum response body size that can be submitted inline

storageUploadRequest.description: Pre-signed request for uploading large response bodies

ScheduledEvent.description:
Represents a scheduled event trigger, typically from a cron job or timer.

This struct aims to provide a common representation for scheduled events
across different providers like AWS CloudWatch Events, Google Cloud Scheduler,
and Azure Timer Triggers.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the event was scheduled or triggered.

ServiceAccount.description:
Represents a non-human identity that can be assumed by compute services
such as Lambda, Cloud Run, ECS, Container Apps, etc.

Maps to:
- AWS: IAM Role
- GCP: Service Account
- Azure: User-assigned Managed Identity

The ServiceAccount is automatically created from permission profiles in the stack
and contains the resolved permission sets for both stack-level and resource-scoped access.

id.description:
Identifier for the service account. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

stackPermissionSets.description:
Stack-level permission sets that apply to all resources in the stack.
These are derived from the "*" scope in the permission profile.
Resource-scoped permissions are handled by individual resource controllers.

ServiceAccountOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned ServiceAccount.

identity.description:
The platform-specific identifier of the service account
- AWS: Role ARN
- GCP: Service Account email
- Azure: Managed Identity client ID

resourceId.description:
The platform-specific resource name/ID
- AWS: Role name
- GCP: Service Account unique ID
- Azure: Managed Identity resource ID

Stack.description: A bag of resources, unaware of any cloud.

permissions.description: Combined permissions configuration containing both profiles and management

resources.description: Map of resource IDs to their configurations and lifecycle settings

oneOf.description: Reference to the current stack being built
Reference to another stack by ID

external.description: Reference to another stack by ID

StackRef.description: Reference to a stack for management permissions

StackResourceState.description: Represents the state of a single resource within the stack for a specific platform.

_internal.description:
The platform-specific resource controller that manages this resource's lifecycle.
This is None when the resource status is Pending.
Stored as JSON to make the struct serializable and movable to alien-core.

dependencies.description:
Complete list of dependencies for this resource, including infrastructure dependencies.
This preserves the full dependency information from the stack definition.

oneOf.description: Stores the last error encountered during a failed step transition.

isExternallyProvisioned.description:
True if the resource was provisioned by an external system (e.g., CloudFormation).
Defaults to false, indicating dynamic provisioning by the executor.

lastFailedState.description:
Stores the controller state that failed, used for manual retry operations.
This allows resuming from the exact point where the failure occurred.
Stored as JSON to make the struct serializable and movable to alien-core.

oneOf.description:
The lifecycle of the resource (Frozen, Live, LiveOnSetup).
Defaults to Live if not specified.
Outputs generated by the resource (e.g., ARN, URL, Bucket Name).

The previous resource configuration during updates.
This is set when an update is initiated and cleared when the update completes or fails.

remoteBindingParams.description:
Binding parameters for remote access.
Only populated when the resource has `remote_access: true` in its ResourceEntry.
This is the JSON serialization of the binding configuration (e.g., StorageBinding, VaultBinding).
Populated by controllers during provisioning using get_binding_params().

retryAttempt.description: Tracks consecutive retry attempts for the current state transition.

status.description: High-level status derived from the internal state.

type.description: The high-level type of the resource (e.g., Function::RESOURCE_TYPE, Storage::RESOURCE_TYPE).

StackSettings.description:
User-customizable deployment settings specified at deploy time.

These settings are provided by the customer via CloudFormation parameters,
Terraform attributes, CLI flags, or Helm values. They customize how the
agent is deployed and what capabilities are enabled.

**Key distinction**: StackSettings is user-customizable, while ManagementConfig
is platform-derived (from the Agent Manager's ServiceAccount).

deploymentModel.description:
Deployment model: push (Agent Manager) or pull (Operator).
Default: Push for cloud platforms.
Kubernetes and Local platforms only support Pull.

heartbeats.description:
How heartbeat health checks are handled.
- off: No heartbeat permissions
- on: Heartbeat enabled (default)

oneOf.description:
Network configuration for the stack (VPC/VNet settings).
If `None`, an isolated VPC with NAT is auto-created when the stack has resources
that require networking (e.g., containers). Set explicitly to customize:
`UseDefault` for the provider's default network (fast, dev/test only),
`Create` for an isolated VPC with managed NAT (production), or `ByoVpc*`
to reference an existing customer-managed VPC.

telemetry.description:
How telemetry (logs, metrics, traces) is handled.
- off: No telemetry permissions
- auto: Telemetry flows automatically (default)
- approval-required: Telemetry waits for explicit approval

updates.description:
How updates are delivered.
- auto: Updates deploy automatically (default)
- approval-required: Updates wait for explicit approval

StackState.description: Represents the collective state of all resources in a stack, including platform and pending actions.

platform.description: The target platform for this stack state.

resourcePrefix.description: A prefix used for resource naming to ensure uniqueness across deployments.

resources.description: The state of individual resources, keyed by resource ID.

StackStatus.description: Represents the overall status of a stack based on its resource states.

id.description:
Name of the the storage bucket.
For names with dots, each dot-separated label must be ≤ 63 characters.

lifecycleRules.description:
List of rules for automatic object management (e.g., expiration).
Default: `[]` (empty list)

publicRead.description:
Allows public read access to objects without authentication.
Default: `false`

StorageEvent.description:
Represents an event triggered by an action in an object storage service.

This struct provides a generic representation for events from services like
AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage (GCS), and Azure Blob Storage.

bucketName.description: The name of the bucket or container where the event occurred.

contentType.description: Optional content type (MIME type) of the object.

copySource.description: Optional information about the source object for copy events.

currentTier.description: Optional current storage tier for TierChanged or Restored events.

etag.description: Optional ETag or hash of the object content.

metadata.description: Optional metadata associated with the object.

objectKey.description: The key or path of the object involved in the event.

previousTier.description: Optional previous storage tier for TierChanged or Restored events.

region.description: Optional region where the event originated.

size.description: Optional size of the object in bytes.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the event occurred.

versionId.description: Optional version or sequencer identifier for the event or object state.

StorageEventType.description: Represents the type of storage event that occurred.

StorageEvents.description: A wrapper type for a list of storage events.

StorageOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned Storage bucket.

bucketName.description: The globally unique name of the bucket.

putRequest.description: Pre-signed request for uploading command params

SubmitResponseRequest.description: Request to submit a command response (from deployment)

TelemetryMode.description: How telemetry (logs, metrics, traces) is handled.

binaryName.description: Name of the binary to build and run

oneOf.description: TypeScript/JavaScript compiled to single executable with Bun

binaryName.description: Name of the compiled binary (defaults to package.json name if not specified)

ToolchainConfig.description:
Configuration for different programming language toolchains.
Each toolchain provides type-safe build configuration and auto-detection capabilities.

UpdatesMode.description: How updates are delivered to the agent.

UploadCompleteRequest.description: Request to mark upload as complete

Vault.description:
Represents a secure vault for storing secrets.
This resource provides a platform-agnostic interface over cloud-native secret management services:
- AWS: AWS Secrets Manager with prefixed secret names
- GCP: Secret Manager with prefixed secret names
- Azure: Key Vault resource

The vault acts as a namespace for secrets and controls access permissions for functions and services.

id.description:
Unique identifier for the vault. Must contain only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores ([A-Za-z0-9-_]).
Maximum 64 characters.

VaultOutputs.description: Outputs generated by a successfully provisioned Vault.

vaultId.description:
Platform-specific vault identifier.
- AWS: Account and region (e.g., "123456789012:us-west-2") - implicit reference to Secrets Manager
- GCP: Project and location (e.g., "projects/my-project/locations/us-central1") - implicit reference to Secret Manager
- Azure: Key Vault resource ID (e.g., "/subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/.../providers/Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/my-vault")
